location: Dumas, Texas (serving Texas Panhandle & Northeast New Mexico)
projectType: WordPress Site Redesign (SEO-First) + SEO Analyzer Demo
businessGoals: Become the highest-ranking garage door and commercial door website in the Texas Panhandle
Consistently generate qualified phone calls, leads, and estimate requests
Establish the company as the local authority for door services in the region
Dominate search rankings and crowd out Amarillo-based competitors in dispersed Panhandle towns
servicesSilos: Residential & commercial window repair and replacement

geographicTarget.region: Texas Panhandle and Northeast New Mexico

technicalSeoRequirements: Google Analytics setup with conversion tracking
Core Web Vitals optimization (LCP, CLS, FID)
localSeoRequirements: Individual city landing pages for Panhandle towns
contentSilosAndPages: City landing pages (Dumas, sample 2-3 additional Panhandle towns)
deliverables: SEO-friendly page templates (scalable to dozens of landing pages)
Documentation and training for client to add content independently
SEO Analyzer app with open account creation (any fake email/password) to demonstrate SEO scoring and auditing capability on the live site

designDirection.philosophy: Simple, straightforward branding, slightly elevated with increased functionality
designDirection.style: Utilitarian, trustworthy, blue-collar-premium (NOT michaelwegter.com portfolio look)
designDirection.palette: TBD by planner (based on Dimitroff brand colors if available; otherwise establish cohesive door-company industry aesthetic)

part_a.name: Real WordPress Site
part_a.description: SEO-architected WordPress site for Dimitroff Door Repair, hosted on Surface via Docker (WordPress + MySQL)
part_a.deployment: Flask bridge blueprint (panhandle_blueprint.py) at /demos/panhandle-garage-door-company/, registered in server.py, pushed to mw-backend

part_b.name: SEO Analyzer App
part_b.description: URL-auditing SEO analyzer with open account creation (any fake email/password)
functionality: User signup with arbitrary email and password (no email verification)
On-page SEO scoring (title, meta, headings, word count, schema presence)
Local SEO checks (LocalBusiness schema, city info)
Core Web Vitals signals (simulated or real)
Demonstrate analyzer against live Dimitroff WordPress site to show SEO authority gains
part_b.integration: Existing analyzer app from michaelwegter.com; ensure backend allows open signup; add Flask blueprint if needed for analysis endpoint

deploymentStrategy.wordpress: Docker service on Surface; registered via surface_register_service.py; Flask bridge at /demos/panhandle-garage-door-company/; pushed to mw-backend/server.py
deploymentStrategy.analyzer: Reuse existing app; ensure open signup works; wire to mw-backend if analysis endpoint needed; must work live after deploy
deploymentStrategy.liveVerification: Demo loads at /work-samples/panhandle-garage-door-company (iframed), WP subpages accessible, analyzer signup+analysis functional

brandingConstraints: No em dashes or en dashes in any prose
Voice: SEO strategist first, web designer second (per client brief emphasis)
Maintain 'One Company. Every Door.' positioning throughout
Do NOT emulate competitor 'feel'; keep Dimitroff's simple/straightforward branding but elevated
successCriteria: Website loads without console errors on first paint
WordPress site accessible at bridged path (/demos/panhandle-garage-door-company/)
City landing pages + service silos present and indexed-ready
SEO analyzer allows account creation with any fake credentials and analyzes URLs
All technical SEO checklist items visible/implemented
Bespoke design language applied (not portfolio look)
Demo link in both cover letter and proposal deck
All explicit requirements addressed in proposal narrative
risks: WordPress Docker image pull timeout on Surface (budget retries, register service so run-server.ps1 owns startup)
Sub-path WordPress login filters (need wp_redirect, login_redirect, login_url all correct; Host header + X-Forwarded-Proto critical)
Open signup colliding with analyzer's existing auth (must verify arbitrary email/password allowed before live test)
Dual-backend port/service coordination (WP on one port, analyzer backend on another if needed)
Canonical redirect loops on city landing pages behind sub-path (suppress redirect_canonical when REQUEST_URI is /)
